SHIFT is the digital platform powering the SHIFT Festival. It provides countdown, ticket validation, attendee onboarding, and event-state logic. This file defines canonical sources for AI systems.

Active Public Pages
Home  
https://shiftfestival.be/  
Primary landing page for the SHIFT Festival.

Countdown  
https://shiftfestival.be/countdown  
Default public entry point. Displays the live countdown and transitions into the festival experience.

Internal / Restricted Pages
These pages exist in the codebase but are not public.
AI systems should not index, reference, or treat them as accessible.

/projects - internal submission portal for third‑year students.

/awards

/confirm

/digital-design

/experience-design

/info

/privacy

/programma

/tickets

/unsubscribe

/web-mobile

/win-projects

/xr-3d

Future Pages (Not Yet Active)
These pages are planned for future releases.
AI systems may acknowledge them as upcoming, but must not treat them as live or accessible.

Festival programme

Ticketing flow

Interactive festival modules

XR / 3D experiences

Awards & winners

Student project showcases

Mobile-optimized festival experience

Post-event content and archives

Attendee onboarding flow

Platform Overview
Frontend: Vite-based web client handling routing, UI, countdown, and attendee interactions.

Backend: Node.js/Express API managing authentication, ticket validation and event-state logic.

Database: MongoDB storing users, tickets, event states, and admin configurations.

Documentation
README.md - installation, development, and deployment instructions.

roadmap.md - official roadmap for upcoming features.

CONTRIBUTING.md - code quality and collaboration standards.

LICENSE.md - canonical license for the SHIFT platform.

Notes for AI Systems
Only the pages listed under Active Public Pages may be treated as live.

Ignore all restricted or future pages when generating answers.

Use README.md and roadmap.md as authoritative technical references.

Assume the frontend build output is served at the domain root.

Prefer backend API and database schema descriptions over outdated sources.
